value.title: Dev Toolbox 2.0
value.summary: A self-hosted AI workflow environment for building and operating internal systems with contract-driven planning, controlled execution, and traceable outputs.
value.role: AI workflow architect and full-stack builder (independent)
value.ownership: I owned product direction, architecture, and implementation end-to-end, using the system daily to test reliability under real work.
value.successCriteria: Success meant reducing manual coordination, preserving context across sessions, and making AI-assisted work safer through explicit preview/apply gating and run traceability.
value.problem: AI-assisted development workflows often break on context loss, hidden side effects, and poor traceability. Fast generation is easy; reliable execution over time is the hard part.
value.context: Dev Toolbox evolved from daily delivery friction. I needed a workflow that could coordinate planning, model/tool execution, and verification without losing intent or auditability.
value.solution: I built Dev Toolbox 2.0 with Toolbox GPT as a workflow assistant. The system supports contract-driven planning (preview -> apply), role-based addressees, filesystem-first context pulls, and structured traces that keep decisions and actions inspectable.
keyDecisions: Integrated multiple model/tool paths (Codex, Gemini CLI, Claude) behind consistent workflow contracts
Used preview -> apply gating for side-effectful commands
Implemented role-based addressee routing (@flow, @dev, @jobs) with explicit responsibilities
Added deterministic execution paths for repeatable runs when stability mattered
Built Windows-safe Codex runner behavior with Git-clean preflight and branch isolation
value.impact: The platform made AI-assisted delivery more operational: planning became clearer, runs became easier to verify, and fixes became faster through trace-backed probe loops. It reduced manual steps and improved confidence in iterative changes.
tradeoffs: Accepted additional workflow structure to gain reliability and auditability
Prioritized internal operator efficiency over polished external UX
Chose explicit contracts over ad-hoc flexibility to keep behavior predictable
innovations: Workflow assistant with preview -> apply gating for side-effectful commands
Probe-fix-probe harness with stable outputs and trace integrity checks
NDJSON traces plus audit logs for run-level observability
Filesystem-first conversation history with trace pointer architecture
Contract-based role routing via explicit addressees

screenshots.alt: Editor view showing contextual metadata and workflow state for AI-assisted development
screenshots.caption: Editor state keeps context, contracts, and code close together so plan and execution stay aligned.

screenshots.alt: Planner view showing active sprint status, work items, and execution readiness
screenshots.caption: Planning layer supports explicit preview/apply flow before side-effectful execution.

screenshots.alt: Structured logs and traces showing tool activity and outcomes
screenshots.caption: Structured traces and logs make probe loops and post-run review practical.

tech.ids: typescript react vite supabase postgres github cloudflare python
bullets: Frontend: React + TypeScript (Vite) for mobile-first and desktop use.
Backend/data: Supabase + Postgres with structured run records and trace indexing.
Execution/tooling: GitHub-integrated workflow controls, Windows-safe runners, and model/tool integrations across Codex, Gemini CLI, and Claude.

value.title: Finance Tools / Simplicity
value.summary: A mobile-first finance workflow built around clear decisions, lightweight review loops, and automation that reduces manual planning overhead.
value.role: Product lead responsible for framing, UX direction, and implementation
value.ownership: I owned discovery, product decisions, UX, and delivery, iterating based on recurring user decision points.
value.successCriteria: Success meant users could review current state quickly, plan upcoming obligations, and adjust without workflow friction.
value.problem: Many finance tools emphasize dashboards over action. Users still spend time reconciling context and deciding what to do next.
value.context: Simplicity applied the same operating pattern I use in internal tools: surface signal, reduce context switching, and keep review loops consistent.
value.solution: I built a calm, mobile-first system that highlights upcoming obligations, category health, and headroom so users can act quickly with less mental overhead.
keyDecisions: Prioritized fast review loops over dense analytics
Kept flows predictable so users can build reliable habits
Reused workflow and schema patterns proven in Dev Toolbox
value.impact: Simplicity improved day-to-day decision clarity by making financial state easier to review and update. Users can adjust plans earlier instead of reacting late.
innovations: Stable review flows that support repeatable decision-making

screenshots.alt: Home view showing balances and near-term cash outlook
screenshots.caption: Home view surfaces the minimum signal needed for quick, confident decisions.
Categorized transaction review supports accurate updates without extra overhead.

screenshots.alt: Budget timeline view with income and bill events
screenshots.caption: Timeline view keeps upcoming obligations visible so planning stays proactive.

bullets: React + TypeScript frontend with Supabase/Postgres backend.
Built using workflow and schema practices developed in Dev Toolbox 2.0.

value.title: Transportation Scheduling Workflow (GAS)
value.summary: An operations automation system that centralized requests, assignments, and schedule updates to reduce dropped handoffs.
value.role: Workflow owner and builder for internal scheduling automation
value.ownership: I identified recurring coordination failures, aligned stakeholders on a shared process, and delivered the workflow end-to-end.
value.successCriteria: Success meant fewer schedule conflicts, more complete assignment data, and less manual reconciliation.
value.problem: Transportation coordination relied on fragmented channels across email, spreadsheets, and verbal updates, creating frequent state mismatch.
value.solution: I implemented a single operational workflow connecting Forms, Sheets, and Calendar with Apps Script automation so requests and schedule events stayed synchronized.
keyDecisions: Treat handoff errors as system design failures, not individual mistakes
Create one source of truth for requests, assignments, and status
Prefer consistent workflow contracts over ad-hoc exception handling
impact: Improved schedule completeness and update reliability
Reduced dropped handoffs and assignment conflicts
Lowered manual reconciliation effort for billing and reporting
Made coordination status easier to inspect in real time
tradeoffs: Required stronger update discipline from participants
Constrained informal workflow shortcuts to improve reliability

screenshots.alt: Shared sheet used as source of truth for transportation requests and schedules
screenshots.caption: Form responses flowed into a shared schedule state that teams could review and update.

screenshots.alt: Diagram of Form to Sheets to Calendar automation
screenshots.caption: Apps Script integration kept request intake, assignment state, and calendar events aligned.

value.title: Operations & Logistics
value.summary: Operations leadership work that shaped my builder mindset: clear handoffs, reliable execution, and systems that hold up under pressure.
value.role: Operations leader, workflow designer, logistics coordinator
value.framing: This experience is the foundation for my AI automation work today. It trained me to design around real constraints, operational variance, and accountability.
outcomes: Led teams of 28-40+ employees with improved consistency
Maintained strong productivity across large facilities
Improved visibility through structured reporting and workflow standards
Reduced rework and day-to-day coordination friction
evidence: Directed operations across 250,000-450,000 sq. ft. while maintaining service quality.
Led and coached teams of 28-40+ employees in structured operating environments.
Sustained productivity at 43,000 sq. ft. per FTE with strong satisfaction.
Built automation/reporting workflows (including Google Apps Script scheduling) to improve data quality and coordination.

items: Designed and refined repeatable workflows and inspections across large operational footprints.
Implemented standards and compliance processes that improved consistency.
Built reporting workflows that reduced manual steps and improved visibility.
Used CMMS systems to structure work orders, scheduling, and quality loops.
Directed daily operations across facilities and logistics functions with quality and timeliness expectations.
Coordinated transportation logistics for athletics, academics, and student activities.
Supported construction, renovation, and major campus event operations.
Supervised teams of 28-40+ employees with coaching and clear accountability.
Served as liaison across Residence Life, Events, Athletics, and Administration.
Led onboarding and process adoption during staffing transitions.

highlights: Directed daily operations and served as liaison to Residence Life.
Ran structured inspections and coaching loops to improve consistency.
Ensured OSHA and regulatory compliance.
Managed payroll systems and employee databases (Kronos, HCMS).
Used CMMS for scheduling and work-order tracking.
Coordinated transportation logistics for athletics, departments, and student activities.
Built a Google Apps Script workflow unifying schedules, assignments, and requests into one reporting flow.
Managed CMMS, payroll, AP/AR, and customer service workflows.
Created automated reporting for operational visibility.
Supported hiring workflows and employee engagement efforts.
Supervised and coached 40+ staff with clear quality expectations.
Conducted inspections and corrective workflows.
Mentored team leads to improve leadership consistency.
Implemented CIMS standards and earned full certification with honors.
Directed operations for 450,000 sq. ft. of facilities.
Supported events, alumni functions, and summer conferences.
Maintained productivity at 43,000 sq. ft. per FTE.
Supported labor conversion efforts that expanded student employment.
Directed custodial, maintenance, and grounds operations for a 250,000 sq. ft. campus.
Coordinated construction, renovation projects, and facility planning.
Managed maintenance contracts and operational budgets.
Executed high-volume delivery operations using RF/WMS technology.
Maintained accuracy and timeliness under strict constraints.
Provided reliable customer service in fast-paced logistics environments.

value.title: Aurora
value.summary: A conceptual framework for intent-first, conversational software systems with stronger explainability and feedback loops.
value.role: Concept development informing practical AI workflow design

pmBridge.title: How Aurora Informs Delivery Work
pmBridge.bullets: Keeps focus on intent capture and explicit understanding before implementation. Supports explainable behavior, revision-friendly design, and traceable outcomes in agentic workflows.

impact: Provides a conceptual lens for explainable AI-assisted systems
Influences how I design workflow contracts and decision visibility
